mnemonics: GAPS - Gradient-based, Approximation, Population-based, Stochastic - for remembering the four main categories of advanced optimization techniques.
keyPoints: Gradient-based methods (like Conjugate Gradient) use first or second derivative information for efficient search.
Stochastic methods (like Simulated Annealing) use random processes to escape local minima.
Population-based methods (like Genetic Algorithms) evolve a set of solutions to find an optimum.
commonMistakes: Assuming a single optimization algorithm is best for all problems, rather than matching the technique to the problem type.
Neglecting to properly tune algorithm-specific parameters (like cooling schedule or population size), leading to poor performance.
Confusing local search (finding a nearby optimum) with global search (finding the best overall optimum) capabilities of different methods.
